name: Almond Butter Crunch
recipeInstructions:
1. Toast the chopped almonds on a cookie sheet in a moderate oven (375°F) for 10 minutes, or until lightly golden.
2. In a medium-size heavy saucepan, combine butter or margarine, sugar, corn syrup, and water. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, to 300°F on a candy thermometer. A teaspoonful of the mixture will separate into brittle threads when dropped in cold water.
3. Remove from heat and stir in 1 cup of the toasted almonds. Pour into a buttered 13x9x2-inch pan, spreading quickly and evenly. Let cool.
4. Melt chocolate squares in the top of a double boiler over hot water. Spread half the melted chocolate over the candy, sprinkling with 1/4 cup nuts. Let set for about 20 minutes.
5. Turn the candy out onto wax paper. Spread with remaining chocolate and sprinkle with remaining nuts. Let stand until set.
6. Break into pieces.
Makes 1 pound.
